channel.title: First Principles
channel.description: Essays and lecture notes on physics and mathematics.

item.title: Geometric Formulation of Classical Mechanics
item.description: Where does a Hamiltonian live? Mechanics, at its heart, is geometric: configuration space, phase space, the symplectic form, and the road from Poisson brackets to quantization.

item.title: On Least Action
item.description: Nature is lazy in a very precise way. Of all the paths a particle could take, the one it does take makes a certain integral stationary — and everything else follows.

item.title: Why Fourier Series Work
item.description: Sines and cosines are not just convenient — they are the eigenfunctions of the operator that physics keeps handing us. That is the whole secret.
